8	Stefan Georgi

worst droughts in the history of the Southwestern United States. So bad, in
fact, that a few years ago the bureaucrats in the state capital signed a piece
of legislation that diverts all the water from the Colorado River away from

of the population that lives in big cities cut our town off from any fresh water,
and placed an airtight chokehold around our throats and our futures.
(12:10)
Given how desperate the situation is, you may be wondering why I’d ever raise

10	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 5	Copy Example - Survival Offer
grabbed my rifle from the closet and charged right into the black night, but it

(16:33)
Shining my flashlight around the yard, I saw that my worst nightmare had
come true. The small but thriving garden I’d planted lay completely bare. All my
fresh fruits and vegetables had been picked clean, and almost every plant had
been trampled over in the intruders’ hasty escape. All it took was 10 minutes
for the only food source that my family had left to be completely demolished,
leaving me completely helpless to provide for my wife and daughter. I don’t
know if it was another starving farmer who committed the theft, or some meth
head who thought he could sell the vegetables for money, or what, but I do
know that I hope you never have to experience the way it feels to know that
you failed the ones you love the most, and who most depend on you to keep
them safe. It’s like being punched directly in the solar plexus, except that
feeling doesn’t go away. It stays aching for days. Here’s where we have the
turning point, or part of the turning point. He had a traditional solution, but it
didn’t work. Get a garden to feed his family, but then his garden got looted.
What now? This is the start of the trigger point, where things escalate.
(17:30)
After that night, I had no choice but to start using what little money I had left to
buy “groceries” from the one convenience store still open in our town. Nitrate
filled beef jerky, six-month-old potato chips, even Snickers bars. Not only
did these snacks quickly add up in price, they contained hardly any nutrition
whatsoever. Imagine looking at your starving seven-year-old daughter and
noticing that her skin has taken on an odd, pale shade of yellow, or cringing
when you see that your wife’s eyes are beginning to sink into her skull, and
that her rib cage is starting to poke through her t-shirt, and having nothing to
give them, but another handful of stale Planters Peanuts. It was obvious I was
fighting a battle I couldn’t win. I told my wife and daughter that we were going
to have to abandon our property and head for Flagstaff, Arizona, to live with
my cousin and his wife.
(18:11)
It made me sick to know that I would be completely mooching off relatives,
showing up at their front door like beggars with nothing but three hungry
mouths and whatever clothes we could fit in the cab of my truck, but it just
didn’t seem like there was any other choice. That is until I read the newspaper

something on that third page caught my eye. It was a photograph of an old
looking man who had a receding hairline but the most alive eyes I’d ever seen.
Directly below that photo there was a story about how he and a small team
of researchers had developed a breakthrough method for producing a self-
renewing supply of fresh food and water.
(19:40)
Something they planned to use in the world’s most dangerous countries, places
where local militias and gangs had a monopoly on food, and the citizens were
starving. According to the interview with the man, whose name was Professor
F.W. Craven, the system could run in any climate - from a frozen tundra to a
barren desert, only required a few square feet of space to set up, and need only
a few gallons of water to operate. Most incredibly of all, his team were doing
doing it all right in the Imperial Valley, about 15 minutes from where I lived.
My pulse began to race, as I thought about the implications. If they could build
this entire thing in the space about the size of a closet, then perhaps I could
use it to provide nutritious food and water for my family, even during a terrible
drought, and even keep it concealed so that looters, starving neighbors, or
violent drug addicts would have no idea it was there.

12	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 5	Copy Example - Survival Offer
(20:25)
The following day I drove across town to find this professor. I knew it was a
long shot, but when you’re desperate enough, you’ll do whatever it takes to
protect your family. Looking back now, it almost feels like a dream. Me pulling
up the driveway to the small one-story brick house that looked nothing like a
research lab, me knocking on the front door and telling the graying old man
who answered my entire story - how my family was starving, how we were
out of money, how we were out of hope - me staring dumbly as that same
graying old man led me out to his backyard, a space that was about the size
of an SUV, and where I saw nothing but some colorful flowers, a few bushes,
and four small boxes chucked in a corner. This is supposed to hold the key to
provide a permanent supply of fresh food and drinking water.
(21:02)
To the world’s most impoverished countries? I was confused, then I was angry,
as what professor Craven said next sounded like the most bald-faced lie I’d ever
heard. “Through more than a decade of research and testing, we’ve made the
system incredibly efficient. In fact, in the next year this single micro-farm will
yield 1,500 duck and chicken eggs, 12,500 quail eggs, 210 pounds of duck meat,
230 pounds of chicken meat, 175 pounds of rabbit meat, 120 pounds of juicy
tomatoes, 30 pounds of bell peppers, and 110 pounds of cucumbers. Those are
just the vegetables I’ve chosen to grow. You can easily grow pretty much any
produce you want to eat with it. It will also provide gallons of purified drinking
water to a family during an emergency. But the most important thing is that it
costs almost nothing to build, and less than $140 to get the animals, seeds, and
soil you need.
(21:47)
Once you’ve got those animals, seeds, and supplies, you never have to spend
another dime on them again.” My jaw dropped and I stared at Professor

just didn’t get it. “It’s obvious you don’t believe me,” he said. “That’s okay. I
actually get that a lot.” With that, he launched into the incredible explanation
of how this Holy Grail in self-sufficiency works. Basically, he met the sensei,

it works. We’re not really going to have a mechanism of the problem. I think

Those are the big things that I see there. We’ll get to the mechanism in just a

how this Holy Grail in self-sufficiency works. Professor Craven’s system is

conserved, and nothing is wasted. Take for example rabbits living in the wild.
Their waste feeds the plants, the plants grow and feed the rabbits. It’s a self-
sustaining system where everyone benefits. As Professor Craven was telling

Part of it is this ecosystem and a self-sustaining system. As Professor Craven
was telling me this, he walked over to one of the boxes in the corner and
opened it. “Look.” It wasn’t just an old box. It housed four chickens inside of it.
I told him that I was surprised that there were no sounds at all. “There’s a trick
to soundproofing the box so that you will not hear any sounds. Nobody will
know that you’re keeping all these animals here. I keep five types of animals
here - chickens, ducks, rabbits, quails and fish. You don’t need all of these if
you don’t want to. The system is modular, meaning that if you just decided to
rear fish, or just rabbits, you can do so while remaining self-sufficient.” Next,
the professor took me back inside of his study and pulled out a large piece of
drafting paper.
(24:02)
On it was a blueprint showing me Exactly how everything works. In Craven’s
system, all the animals are comfortably housed inside of soundproof boxes.
Using a few pieces of inexpensive piping and some water, the animal waste
gets transported to the vegetable garden, where it works as an extremely potent
fertilizer. As the water passes through the rock bed that lines his vegetable
garden, it’s naturally purified, and then cycled into the fish tank, although
some of it can also be diverted to containers for clean drinking water during
an emergency. Finally, the fish waste is pumped out of the tank and back to
the vegetable garden. Plus, the vegetables can be used to feed those animals
I mentioned at the start. In other words, this “mad professor” had created
a zero-waste system for growing fresh, juicy fruits and vegetables, while
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

14	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 5	Copy Example - Survival Offer
discreetly raising as many small animals as you want, providing a permanent
supply of fresh meat and protein rich eggs.
(24:49)
Once Craven explained it to me, it made a lot of sense, but I still did have one
major nagging question: Why was the professor obsessed with making this
food and water production system so discreet? The answer, it turns out, is that
he had developed this self-replenishing micro-farm for some of the world’s
most war-torn places, rural villages, and countries like Sudan, Ethiopia and
Afghanistan. The tragic fact of the matter is that in violent places like these
tribal warlords have a monopoly on all food coming in and out of the villages.
They’ll routinely steal food from the local villagers to feed their own armies,
even if it meant leaving innocent and helpless local families to starve. If the
professor was going to start testing the system by providing it to a few villagers,
it was vital that the guerrilla armies and warlords didn’t know it existed.
(25:32)
I thought back to my farm, and how our fruit and vegetables had been taken
in the night, probably by some formerly honest farmer who had been forced to
steal to keep his children from starving. I thought about how my situation now is
exactly the same one that millions of American families would find themselves
in if rising food costs caused the food system to collapse, or the government
broke down and bloody anarchy spilled out into the streets, or even if there was
another major disaster like Katrina. I realized that Professor Craven’s system
was something every single American needed to know about if they wanted to

this repetition here. Choose option three, choose option three, choose option
three. It’s a sneaky NLP going on here. I’m being repetitive on purpose and
making them repeat that they should pick option three. Ultimately the choice
is yours. Whatever you decide you must choose. This presentation is ending
as I speak. Again, urgency number two, presentation’s ending, no guarantee
how long it will be available. This presentation is ending as I speak, and if
you return to it later, I can’t guarantee the $37 price, or the bonuses, or the
guarantee will still be here.
(50:35)
Here’s the final thing I’ll leave you with it, and it’s something you already know
inside of your heart. Self-Sufficiency Shortcuts isn’t really about having food
and water. It’s about taking care of your family. For me, there’s no better
feeling in the world than knowing that no matter what happens, if there’s a
hurricane and the stores are empty, or a dirty bomb contaminates the water
supply, or an EMP causes the grid to go down, I have the peace of mind of
knowing 	my 	completely 	hidden, 	completely 	self-replenishing 	micro-farm
will still be running on autopilot, ensuring that my wife and my little girl are
radiantly healthy and well fed. That’s why I’m so passionate about getting this
simple system into your hands today, so you can finally know that no matter
what happens, your family’s most vital needs are covered. But I can only help
you if you take action right now.
(51:13)
There are only 10 seconds left until this presentation ends. Make the only real
decision there is and click the “add to cart” button you see below. I can’t wait
to hear from you after you’ve gone through the program and find out how quick,
easy and cheap it is to become self-sufficient in just a few square feet of space.
Thank you for listening to my presentation. See you on the other side. Steven.
Again, third call to action, thank you for watching or reading. Now we do the
FAQs. Back to being their friend, helping to clear up any confusion, and remind
them of what the product is, what it’s used for, why they should use it, why they
need to take action today, all that. Does Self-Sufficiency Shortcuts truly run on
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

27	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 5	Copy Example - Survival Offer
autopilot? What kind of food can I reasonably expect to produce in the system?
(51:54)
A chicken coop is loud and smelly. How is it possible that you can have live
animals, not to mention fish, and have it be completely hidden from plain view?
What do I get when I invest in it? What’s included? What happens if I get stuck?
Support. What happens if I change my mind? How do I get started? Another one I
should have probably put here is this thing about: what if I live in an apartment or
a small space, or have roommates? That would have been a good one to put as
well. Reading 8,000 words is not fun for my voice, but I really like this letter a lot.
Maybe the reason it didn’t go live is it was too big of a promise and the product
wasn’t really fully buildable. I think it’s a cool letter, cool offer. It was just another
fun example to do, so hopefully you enjoyed it as well.
